channel.title: Stockwatch - Key Events
channel.description: Real-time corporate announcements, deals, and financial results.

item.title: [VIKASLIFE] Vikas Lifecare Board Approves Q2 & Q3 FY26 Results, Postal Ballot Addendum
item.description:
Vikas Lifecare Ltd's Board of Directors met on April 21, 2026, and approved the Un-audited Financial Results (Standalone and Consolidated) for the quarter and half year ended September 30, 2025, and for the quarter and nine months ended December 31, 2025. These results were reviewed by the Audit Committee and subjected to Limited Review by the Statutory Auditors. Additionally, the Board approved seeking members' consent through an addendum to the postal ballot notice for undertaking various transactions, including related party transactions, investments, loans, guarantees, and creation of charges/mortgages, within limits approved by members, to facilitate efficient business operations.

  • Board approved unaudited financial results for Q2 and H1 FY26.
  • Unaudited financial results for Q3 and nine months ended Dec 31, 2025, also approved.
  • Results for both periods underwent Limited Review by Statutory Auditors.
  • Board approved seeking members' consent via postal ballot addendum.
  • Addendum covers related party transactions, investments, loans, and guarantees.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Vikas Lifecare Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [VIKASLIFE] Vikas Lifecare Board Approves Unaudited Financial Results
item.description:
Vikas Lifecare Ltd's Board of Directors approved the unaudited financial results for the quarter and half year ended September 30, 2025, and the quarter and nine months ended December 31, 2025. The results, both standalone and consolidated, were reviewed and recommended by the Audit Committee and subjected to Limited Review by the Statutory Auditors. The board also approved seeking members’ consent through an addendum to the postal ballot notice for certain transactions.

  • Board approved unaudited financial results for quarter and half year ended September 30, 2025.
  • Board approved unaudited financial results for quarter and nine months ended December 31, 2025.
  • Results were reviewed by the Audit Committee and subjected to Limited Review by Statutory Auditors.
  • Approval sought for members’ consent via addendum to postal ballot for business transactions.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Vikas Lifecare Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [VIKASLIFE] Vikas Lifecare Board Approves Unaudited Financial Results
item.description:
The Board of Directors of Vikas Lifecare Ltd approved the unaudited financial results for the quarter and half year ended September 30, 2025, and the quarter and nine months ended December 31, 2025. These results, both standalone and consolidated, were reviewed and recommended by the Audit Committee and subjected to Limited Review by the Statutory Auditors. The board also approved seeking members’ consent through an addendum to the postal ballot notice for certain transactions.

  • Unaudited financial results approved for quarter/half year ended September 30, 2025.
  • Unaudited financial results approved for quarter/nine months ended December 31, 2025.
  • Approval sought for members’ consent via addendum to postal ballot notice.
  • Limited Review Reports enclosed for both standalone and consolidated results.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Vikas Lifecare Ltd | Category: Board Meeting

item.title: [BIRET] Brookfield India REIT closes unit issue at ₹323.00, 2.10% discount
item.description:
Brookfield India Real Estate Trust REIT's Issue Committee approved the closure of its institutional placement of units on April 21, 2026. The issue price was determined at ₹323.00 per unit, which includes a discount of ₹6.94 (2.10%) to the floor price of ₹329.94. The committee also approved the placement document dated April 21, 2026, and finalized the confirmation of allocation notes for successful eligible institutional investors.

  • Institutional placement of units closed on April 21, 2026.
  • Issue price set at ₹323.00 per unit.
  • Price includes a 2.10% discount to the floor price.
  • Placement document and allocation notes were approved.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Brookfield India Real Estate Trust REIT | Category: Corporate Action

item.title: [TIDEWATER] Veedol Corporation Ltd: Sanjeev Wangoo Resigns as Executive Director
item.description:
Veedol Corporation Limited announced the resignation of Mr. Sanjeev Wangoo from his role as Executive Director – Group Supply Chain Management (SMP), effective April 17, 2026. The company made this disclosure in accordance with Regulation 30 of the SEBI (Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements) Regulations, 2015. Mr. Wangoo's resignation is attributed to personal and professional reasons, and he has committed to ensuring a smooth transition of his responsibilities.

  • Sanjeev Wangoo resigned as Executive Director, Group Supply Chain Management.
  • Resignation is effective April 17, 2026.
  • Reasons cited for resignation are personal and professional.
  • The company will ensure a smooth transition of responsibilities.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: TIDE WATER OIL (INDIA) LTD. | Category: Management Change

item.title: [SUNTECK] Sunteck Realty: No Deviation in Preferential Issue Fund Use
item.description:
Sunteck Realty Ltd has submitted its Monitoring Agency Report for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, in compliance with SEBI regulations. India Ratings & Research Private Limited, the monitoring agency, confirmed "no deviation from the objects" regarding the utilization of proceeds from the preferential issue of warrants convertible into equity shares. The preferential issue, conducted from November 28 to December 5, 2025, involved 1,17,64,705 share warrants at ₹425 each, raising ₹499.99 Crores. The report is based on management undertakings and documents, with the agency noting no independent verification was performed.

  • Monitoring Agency Report for Q4 FY26 submitted for Sunteck Realty.
  • India Ratings & Research found no deviation in fund utilization.
  • Report pertains to ₹499.99 Cr preferential issue of warrants.
  • Issue occurred between November 28 and December 5, 2025.
  • Compliance with SEBI (ICDR) Regulations, 2018 confirmed.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SUNTECK REALTY LTD. | Category: Regulatory

item.title: [CRISIL] CRISIL receives ₹148.99 Cr income tax demand; plans appeal
item.description:
🔥 Trending

CRISIL Ltd has received an income tax assessment order dated April 21, 2026, for the financial year 2022-23 (AY 2023-24). The order, issued under Section 143(3) read with Section 144B of the Income-tax Act, proposes certain disallowances by the assessing officer. This has resulted in a tax plus interest demand of ₹148.99 Crores. The company has stated that there is no immediate financial or operational impact and intends to file an appeal against the order.

  • CRISIL received an income tax assessment order for FY23.
  • Order includes a tax and interest demand of ₹148.99 Cr.
  • Company plans to appeal against the assessment order.
  • No immediate financial or operational impact expected.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: CRISIL LTD. | Category: Tax & Penalty

item.title: [RELINFRA] Reliance Infra: ₹670.48 Cr Provisional Asset Attachment Confirmed
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Reliance Infrastructure Ltd. has received an order dated April 20, 2026, from the Adjudicating Authority, PMLA, confirming the provisional attachment of certain company assets. The attached assets amount to ₹670.48 crore and relate to alleged violations of the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA) during the period from 2017 to 2019. The company intends to file an appeal to challenge this attachment. Despite the order, Reliance Infrastructure has stated that there will be no impact on its ongoing business operations. This disclosure is in compliance with SEBI regulations.

  • PMLA Adjudicating Authority confirmed provisional attachment of ₹670.48 crore assets.
  • Attachment pertains to alleged PMLA violations from 2017 to 2019.
  • Reliance Infrastructure plans to appeal the attachment order.
  • Company asserts no impact on its business operations.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: RELIANCE INFRASTRUCTURE LTD. | Category: Legal

item.title: [CMPDI] CMPDI achieves 105% drilling target, approves MECL MoU & REE block capex
item.description:
🔥 Trending

Central Mine Planning & Design Institute Ltd (CMPDI) announced key outcomes from its Board Meeting. The company achieved 105% of its drilling target for FY 2025-26, completing 11.505 lakh meters against a target of 11.00 lakh meters, marking a 14% growth. Seismic survey targets were also surpassed by 101%. The Board approved a new five-year Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Mineral Exploration Corporation Ltd (MECL) for Energy & Non-Energy Mineral Exploration, effective from April 1, 2026, continuing a partnership dating back to FY 2008-09. Additionally, CMPDI secured the Nawatala Devigarh REE block in Rajasthan through auction, offering a 36.45% revenue share. The Board approved a capital expenditure of ₹5.43 crore for the G4 stage exploration of this Rare Earth Element (REE) block in FY 2026-27, part of an estimated total exploration expenditure of ₹24.88 crore over five years. CMPDI is eligible for 50% reimbursement of eligible exploration expenditure from NMEDT.

  • Achieved 105% of drilling target (11.505 lakh m) and 101% of seismic survey target for FY25-26.
  • Approved new 5-year MoU with MECL for energy and non-energy mineral exploration.
  • Secured Nawatala Devigarh REE block in Rajasthan with 36.45% revenue share.
  • Board approved ₹5.43 crore capex for G4 stage exploration of REE block in FY26-27.
  • Eligible for 50% reimbursement of exploration costs from NMEDT for the REE block.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: Central Mine Planning & Design Institute Ltd | Category: Business Update

item.title: [SUNTECK] Sunteck Realty: No Deviation in Preferential Issue Funds Use for Q4 FY26
item.description:
Sunteck Realty Ltd. confirmed no deviation or variation in the utilization of proceeds from its preferential issue of warrants for the quarter ended March 31, 2026. The company raised ₹499.999 Crores on December 5, 2025, through a preferential issue of warrants. The statement, reviewed by the Audit Committee on April 21, 2026, indicated that funds were allocated for land acquisition (₹188 Crores), project deployment (₹187.99 Crores), and general corporate purposes (₹124 Crores). During the quarter, ₹11.25 Crores was received and fully utilized from the exercise of 3,52,941 convertible warrants.

  • No deviation in preferential issue warrant proceeds for Q4 FY26.
  • Company raised ₹499.999 Crores via warrants on Dec 5, 2025.
  • Audit Committee reviewed and confirmed no variations in fund use.
  • Funds allocated for land acquisition, project deployment, and general corporate purposes.
  • ₹11.25 Crores utilized from warrant exercise during Q4 FY26.

📄 Read Official Filing (PDF)


Company: SUNTECK REALTY LTD. | Category: Regulatory
